prompt:
Every time somebody on the team has to hand a gnarly command line to someone else — filenames with spaces, apostrophes, tabs, the works — they hand-quote it in Slack and it arrives broken. zsh already knows how to quote things three different ways; I want a tiny inspection tool that makes those renderings visible and copy-pasteable: `argshow.zsh` in the repo root, zsh 5.9, no external commands.

Modes (the mode flag, if any, is the first argument):

1. Report mode (default): for each word given on the command line, print a five-line block. Lines are `[N] tag: <rendering>` — N is the 1-based word index, a single space after the colon, and the rendering sits between < and > so trailing whitespace is visible. Tags in order:
   - `raw` — the word exactly as received (a word containing a newline makes the block span extra lines; that's fine and wanted),
   - `q` — the word as `${(q)word}` renders it (backslash-escaped form),
   - `qq` — as `${(qq)word}` renders it (single-quoted form),
   - `q-` — as `${(q-)word}` renders it (minimal form: bare when safe, quoted when not),
   - `rt` — `ok` if unquoting each of the three renderings with the `(Q)` flag gives back the raw word, otherwise `MISMATCH`.
   No words: print nothing, exit 0.
2. `--line word...`: render every word in the minimal `(q-)` form and print them joined with single spaces as ONE line — the copy-pasteable reconstruction of the argv. No words: print just an empty line.
3. `--unquote word...`: the inverse — each argument is a quoted rendering (any of the three forms); strip one level of quoting with `(Q)` and print `<result>` one per line.

Anything else starting with `--` as the first argument: print `usage: argshow.zsh [--line|--unquote] [word ...]` to stderr and exit 2, nothing to stdout.

The acceptance tests pin the rendered forms byte-for-byte (zsh 5.9's own output for those flags) and round-trip the --line output through eval to prove the reconstruction is faithful — words with `$`, globs, quotes, tabs, newlines, backslashes, and the empty word all have to come back identical.

Acceptance: `zsh test_argshow.zsh` from the workspace root must pass unmodified. Do not modify the test file.
